themes.exampleQuote: One of the widest tea selections I have ever seen
a wonderful pseudo-'third space' where you can sip tea, relax, play a board game, study, and talk to other patrons
It's hard to get a seat sometimes because it's crowded
Staff is extremely friendly and knowledgeable about tea
The babaganoush is delicious! I have been back and tried some food

notable_quote: One of my favorite places on Earth: a wonderfully large selection of teas from around the world, served in an intimate and quiet setting.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers return obsessively for the tea depth, the bell-on-the-table service ritual, and a room that accommodates first dates and solo study sessions equally; the only consistent friction is seating scarcity during peak hours.
quarter_narrative: Ten separate Pittsburgh creators posted to TikTok about Dobra Tea this quarter, generating nearly 10,000 plays with zero posts from the business itself on that platform. The Bombay chai alone pulled 2,589 plays in a single video. Meanwhile, on Instagram, one post went up in 30 days and no reels were published, leaving the business well below the family median for Instagram momentum at a moment when Allegheny Coffee and Tea Exchange, the Pittsburgh Cafes family leader this issue, built its top ranking largely on social momentum. Dobra's review signal is the strongest anchor it has, 1,107 Google reviews at 82 percent five-star, and that foundation keeps it at number one in the tea house category. Moving up in the broader Cafes family ranking depends on whether the visual and social signals can close the gap that the review sentiment has already opened.
tldr_read: Dominant review sentiment, nearly dormant social output. Ones to Watch, #1 in Pittsburgh Tea Houses.
tldr_meaning: The review ceiling has been reached; the remaining climb inside the Cafes family is a function of Instagram momentum and creator signal conversion, two axes that are currently well below the family median.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: First In Tea On The Strength Of 1,107 Reviews. The Social Signal Has Not Kept Pace.

playbook.headline: Restart Instagram with weekly reels
playbook.action: Zero reels published in 30 days; one short-form video per week tied to a specific tea, the Bombay chai is already proven bait, closes the gap to the family median momentum signal.

playbook.headline: Capture the creators already filming
playbook.action: Ten creators posted 11 TikToks this quarter without any outreach from the business; a simple repost or story share of the top-performing videos converts existing coverage into owned reach.

playbook.headline: Expand tagged photo categories on Google
playbook.action: With 690 photos across only six labeled categories, adding staff-submitted images to underrepresented sections, particularly food and drink close-ups, pushes the visual catalog above the family median of 77.
